reasoning: Based on comprehensive evidence up to October 26, 2025: (1) Markets pricing shows very high probability (97% per CME FedWatch) of 25bp cut from 4.00%-4.25% to 3.75%-4.00%; (2) Powell stated on Oct 16 that despite government shutdown data gaps, employment and inflation outlooks 'don't appear to have changed much,' signaling Fed is proceeding; (3) Fed minutes show officials agreed job market risks warranted cuts; (4) While internal divisions exist (Bowman/Waller dissented previously), the consensus for easing remains strong; (5) Government shutdown creates data challenges but Powell indicated Fed can rely on alternative data sources. The weight of evidence strongly favors a 25bp cut, with the main risk being internal dissent rather than policy reversal.
